expectations.text: Names a specific relationalizer technique (procedural macros, derive macros, or compile-time type-directed serialization) and ties it to the Rust type system
expectations.evidence: spytial-derive section: '#[derive(Spytial)] walks the struct/enum at compile time and generates impl Spytial' plus a complete attribute-macro family.

expectations.text: Addresses identity for Rust types — distinguishes value semantics from references (&, Box, Rc, Arc), or notes that struct field paths can serve as stable IDs
expectations.evidence: Identity bullet: 'Use raw-pointer addresses for &T walks of owned structures, plus a user-overridable IdentityHook... For Vec/Box/Rc/Arc you should special-case.' Item 2 in 'Things to decide' adds structural identity for Copy/Clone types.

expectations.text: Drafts at least one concrete macro/attribute signature, e.g. #[orientation(...)] / #[align(...)] / #[group(...)] / #[derive(SpytialDecorators)]
expectations.evidence: spytial-derive section: #[spytial::orientation(selector=..., directions=[...])], #[spytial::group(...)], #[spytial::atom_color(...)], #[spytial::cyclic(...)], plus a v1 list of 15 attribute macros.

expectations.text: Recommends emitting JSON conforming to JSONDataInstance (Path A) over implementing IDataInstance directly (Path B)
expectations.evidence: Recommends 'emit YAML for cnd_spec to stay byte-compatible with Spytial's frontend' and reusing 'the existing copeanddrag npm renderer unchanged.' Never names JSONDataInstance, never discusses Path A vs Path B from custom-data-instance.md. The agent appears to have only found the Python sPyTial / Cope-and-Drag stack, not spytial-core's IDataInstance contract.

expectations.text: Addresses Subproblem 4 (Gaps) for at least one of the user's examples
expectations.evidence: Vec<Person> section mentions 'one atom per element, an index relation if you want ordering' — addresses ordering. Item 2 in 'Things to decide' addresses Rc/Arc cycles and value duplication.

expectations.text: Mentions Caraspace or the existing Rust integration as a reference / precedent
expectations.evidence: No mention of Caraspace anywhere. The agent did not find the existing Rust integration in its WebSearches; it consulted the Python sPyTial repo and Cope and Drag instead.

expectations.text: States how the diagram will surface (browser tab, explicit diagram() call, file output) — Subproblem 3
expectations.evidence: spytial runtime crate section: 'Diagram then has .open_in_browser(), .to_html(path), .to_json() — analogues of Spytial's diagram(..., method=file).'

expectations.text: Provides a path to a minimum-viable build (JSON emitter + HTML harness for one example), not just abstract design
expectations.evidence: 'What I'd build first' section: a 200-line spike defining trait Spytial + InstanceBuilder, hand-implementing for the binary tree, emitting (json, yaml) and piping through CnD.
